spec_match.rationale: The demo now uses the CSS Values 5 request modifier spelling, including cross-origin(anonymous) rather than the obsolete crossorigin() text, and ties the font scenario to a live FontFace load attempt.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified the 'Test Font Load' button fetches a CORS-enabled WOFF2, computes a SHA-256 SRI value, loads a control font with new FontFace(), observes document.fonts.load(), and reports the native modifier fallback when this Chrome build cannot parse integrity() inside FontFace sources.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligned with the parent index's description of demonstrating hash verification, cascade failure, and hash mismatches.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers hash format regex validation, fetch/CORS failure handling within the SHA-256 calculator, successful FontFace control loading, and unsupported native modifier parsing as an explicit fallback state.

references.rationale: Includes direct links to sibling modifier demos, the parent index, ChromeStatus, the CSS Values 5 request URL modifiers section, and the W3C Subresource Integrity specification.

design_system.rationale: Flawless implementation of design rules, including consistent list items, borders, colors, and layout spacing.

summary: DevTools MCP reproduced the original gap at /v150/css-url-request-modifiers/integrity-chain-demo/?verify=before-fontface-live: the Test Font Load button only displayed static support copy, made no FontFace or document.fonts.load() call, created no resource requests, lacked CSS Values/SRI reference links, and still contained crossorigin() text. The repaired page was tested at /v150/css-url-request-modifiers/integrity-chain-demo/?verify=after-fontface-live-final: the button fetched 31340 WOFF2 bytes from fonts.gstatic.com, computed sha256-LDK5s+41jBGeIQ9vUZX5vTSJTXinhf8uldYOcY5ACvQ=, loaded the control font with FontFace.status=loaded and document.fonts.load() returning 1 face, displayed the native integrity() modifier fallback for the current Chrome build, rendered the control font preview, exposed direct ChromeStatus/CSS Values 5/SRI links, and had no console messages or failed network requests for the exercised path.
